text: Every Friday, please compile weekly release notes from all PRs merged into main since last Friday. Steps: 1) Fetch merged PRs with labels feature/enhancement/bug/breaking-change; 2) Categorize into Features, Improvements, Fixes; 3) Include PR number, title, author handle, and link; 4) Add an Upgrade Notes section for any breaking-change labels; 5) Deliver a clean Markdown doc. Post summary to #release-updates.

Got it. I will fetch merged PRs, categorize by labels, include PR #, title, author, and link, add Upgrade Notes for breaking changes, and format as Markdown with sections: Features, Improvements, Fixes, Upgrade Notes. I will also provide a Slack-ready summary for #release-updates.

Same as last time: weekly release notes. Use the exact sections (Features, Improvements, Fixes, Upgrade Notes). Keep each bullet to a single line: "[#1234] Title — @author (link)". Limit to max 6 bullets per section.
Understood. I will replicate the prior format with the one-line bullet style and a cap of 6 bullets per section.
No, I told you last time to include both PR numbers and links. The last draft missed a few links. Please fix and keep the section order consistent.

Friday again. Pull merged PRs from GitHub since last Friday 00:00 UTC. Tool chain: search PRs → label-based categorization → compile Markdown → post Slack summary. If a PR has both feature and breaking-change, include it under Features and also mention in Upgrade Notes. If a section is empty, omit the heading.

Acknowledged. I will run the same chain: fetch merged PRs → categorize → compile → post. If a PR is both feature and breaking-change, it appears under Features and also gets a note in Upgrade Notes. Empty sections will be omitted.
Reminder: we do this every Friday. Use the exact markdown template and Slack summary format. Also, exclude draft PRs and anything closed without merge.

Quick check: for release notes, if a PR has neither feature nor enhancement nor bug labels, place it under Improvements only if it touches docs or internal tooling; otherwise include it at the end under Misc with the same bullet format.
Copy. Default: unlabeled PRs go to Misc unless they are clearly docs or tooling, then Improvements. I will preserve the one-line bullet format and section order.
